title: Ordered categorical boosting as a separate expert model
summary: This dataset has very high-cardinality categorical structure (Driver, Race, Year×Race, Driver×Compound).
rationale: This dataset has very high-cardinality categorical structure (Driver, Race, Year×Race, Driver×Compound). CatBoost’s ordered boosting and ordered target statistics are specifically designed to reduce prediction shift and target leakage from categorical encodings, which is relevant here because many strong hand-built features are effectively target-like historical summaries.
implementation_hint: Train a strong CatBoostClassifier expert on mostly raw columns plus a small number of chronology-safe numeric features, preserving categorical columns directly. Compare against the current hand-engineered LightGBM/XGBoost style baseline, then blend only via strict OOF predictions.
expected_effect: May recover signal from driver/race/category interactions with less manual feature engineering and better generalization to unseen driver-race combinations.
risk: If chronology is not respected in permutations or CV, gains can be illusory; CatBoost may also underuse some specialized numeric interactions already engineered into the baseline.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
